findings.title: ruby-saml has multiple critical authentication bypass CVEs
findings.description: ruby-saml 1.18.1 is installed. CVE-2025-25291 and CVE-2025-25292 (CVSS 8.8) allow SAML authentication bypass via parser differentials between REXML and Nokogiri. CVE-2025-66568 and CVE-2025-66567 affect all versions prior to 1.18.0 (including 1.12.4) related to canonicalization exploits. Additionally, CVE-2025-25293 (CVSS 7.7) is a remote DoS flaw. While 1.18.1 patches some of these, verify all CVEs through 2025-66568 are addressed; the latest safe version may be higher.
findings.recommendation: Check if 1.18.1 addresses CVE-2025-66568 and CVE-2025-66567. If not, upgrade to the latest available ruby-saml version immediately. This is critical for any SAML-based authentication in LingoLinq.

findings.title: rack 2.2.21 is behind latest 2.2.x security patch and the 2.2.x branch is in maintenance mode
findings.description: Rack 2.2.22 was released 2026-02-16 with security fixes. Rack 2.2.21 is missing patches for CVE-2025-27610 (path traversal, CVSS 7.5), CVE-2025-25184 and CVE-2025-27111 (log injection), CVE-2025-61919 (memory-exhaustion DoS), and CVE-2025-61780 (information disclosure). The entire 2.2.x branch is in security-only maintenance; Rack 3.1+ is recommended.
findings.recommendation: Immediately update to rack 2.2.22. Plan migration to Rack 3.x when Rails 7.2 compatibility allows. Note: sinatra 3.2.0 constrains rack to ~> 2.2, so upgrading rack to 3.x requires upgrading sinatra first.

findings.title: nokogiri 1.19.0 missing security patches
findings.description: Nokogiri 1.19.0 is installed but 1.19.1+ fixes CVE-2025-30206 (canonicalization failure). Additionally, earlier CVEs CVE-2024-56171 and CVE-2024-55549 (Use After Free) require 1.18.3+ and 1.18.4+ respectively. While 1.19.0 should include those, the canonicalization fix in 1.19.1 is missing.
findings.recommendation: Update nokogiri to 1.19.1 or later to address CVE-2025-30206.

findings.title: REXML 3.4.4 may be vulnerable to DoS
findings.description: REXML is a transitive dependency (via ruby-saml and others). CVE-2025-58767 is a DoS vulnerability when parsing XML with multiple XML declarations, affecting REXML 3.3.3 through 3.4.1. The installed version 3.4.4 should be patched (fix is in 3.4.2+), but verify this is actually resolved in your locked version.
findings.recommendation: Verify that REXML 3.4.4 includes the fix for CVE-2025-58767. If it does, no action is needed for this specific CVE.

findings.title: Sinatra 3.2.0 has known CVEs
findings.description: CVE-2025-61921 is a ReDoS vulnerability through ETag header value generation in Sinatra. CVE-2024-21510 allows open redirect via X-Forwarded-Host header. Since Sinatra is used by Resque for its web UI, this is exploitable if the Resque dashboard is exposed.
findings.recommendation: Update sinatra to the latest patched version. If Resque web UI is not exposed to the internet, the risk is lower but should still be patched.

findings.title: ruby-openai is 2 major versions behind
findings.description: ruby-openai 6.5.0 is installed (pinned to ~> 6.0 in Gemfile). The latest version is 8.3.0. Major versions 7.x and 8.x include GPT-4o, Realtime API, and structured outputs support. Additionally, OpenAI now has an official Ruby SDK (openai gem) which may supersede ruby-openai.
findings.recommendation: Update the Gemfile constraint from '~> 6.0' to '~> 8.0' and test AI board generation functionality. Review the changelog for breaking changes between 6.x and 8.x. Consider evaluating the official OpenAI Ruby SDK as an alternative.

findings.title: Ember.js 3.28 reached end of life in September 2022
findings.description: Ember.js 3.28 was the final 3.x LTS release. Its 54-week security support window ended approximately September 2022. The project has been running without upstream security patches for over 3 years. The latest Ember version is 6.x. ember-data 3.28.0 is similarly EOL.
findings.recommendation: This is the single largest technical debt item. Plan an incremental upgrade path: 3.28 -> 4.x -> 5.x. The existing .claude/skills/ember-3-28-zero-deprecation and ember-refactor-safety skills should help. Prioritize at least reaching Ember 4.x for security patches.

findings.title: ESLint 5.x is severely outdated and EOL
findings.description: ESLint ^5.16.0 is specified in devDependencies. ESLint 5 was released in 2018 and has been end-of-life for years. Even ESLint 8.x reached EOL in October 2024. The project is 4 major versions behind.
findings.recommendation: Upgrade ESLint as part of the Ember upgrade path. ESLint 9.x requires flat config format. For Ember 3.28, ESLint 7.x is the practical maximum. This should be addressed when upgrading Ember.

findings.title: Stripe gem 18.0.0 is behind latest (18.2.0)
findings.description: Stripe 18.0.0 is installed but the Gemfile has no version constraint. The latest version is 18.2.0. Stripe frequently releases updates with new API features and deprecations. Falling behind can cause issues when Stripe deprecates older API versions.
findings.recommendation: Run 'bundle update stripe' to get the latest 18.x version. Consider pinning to '~> 18.0' in the Gemfile to prevent unexpected major version jumps.

findings.title: coffee-rails is effectively abandoned (last release 2019)
findings.description: coffee-rails 5.0.0 was last released in April 2019 (nearly 7 years ago). CoffeeScript itself is no longer actively developed. This gem adds unnecessary complexity and a build dependency on execjs. CoffeeScript is not recommended for new development.
findings.recommendation: Convert any remaining .coffee files to plain JavaScript or ES6+. Remove the gem from the Gemfile. If no .coffee files exist in the Rails asset pipeline, the gem can be removed immediately.

findings.title: rack-offline is abandoned (last release 2012, uses deprecated HTML5 AppCache)
findings.description: rack-offline 0.6.4 was last released on September 15, 2012 -- over 13 years ago. It generates HTML5 Application Cache (AppCache) manifests, which have been removed from all modern browsers. The technology it supports no longer exists in browsers.
findings.recommendation: Remove rack-offline from the Gemfile. If offline support is needed, use Service Workers instead (which the Ember frontend likely already handles). This gem serves no functional purpose in modern browsers.

findings.title: s3 gem is abandoned (last release ~2014), should use aws-sdk-s3
findings.description: The 's3' gem (version 0.3.29) by qoobaa is an unofficial, unmaintained S3 client. The project already uses aws-sdk-rails, aws-sdk-ses, aws-sdk-sns, aws-sdk-elastictranscoder, and aws-sdk-cloudfront. Using a separate abandoned S3 library creates inconsistency and potential security risks. The gem depends on 'addressable' and 'proxies' (also minimally maintained).
findings.recommendation: Replace the 's3' gem with 'aws-sdk-s3' to align with the rest of the AWS SDK usage in the project. This consolidates the S3 interface, improves security, and removes dependency on an unmaintained library.

findings.title: rails_12factor is unnecessary since Rails 5+
findings.description: rails_12factor 0.0.3 is a Heroku-specific gem that was needed for Rails 4 and earlier. Since LingoLinq has migrated to Render (per project notes) and is on Rails 7.2, this gem is completely unnecessary. Rails 5+ includes the functionality natively via RAILS_SERVE_STATIC_FILES and RAILS_LOG_TO_STDOUT environment variables.
findings.recommendation: Remove rails_12factor from the Gemfile. Ensure RAILS_SERVE_STATIC_FILES and RAILS_LOG_TO_STDOUT environment variables are set in your Render deployment config.

findings.title: sass-rails is deprecated in favor of cssbundling-rails or dartsass-rails
findings.description: sass-rails 6.0.0 depends on sassc-rails, which wraps the deprecated LibSass C library. LibSass is officially deprecated and will not receive new CSS feature support. Modern Rails uses cssbundling-rails or dartsass-rails with the Dart Sass compiler.
findings.recommendation: Since the Ember frontend already uses the 'sass' npm package (Dart Sass) via ember-cli-sass, the Rails asset pipeline usage of sass-rails may be minimal. Audit which Rails views use Sass and plan migration.

findings.title: babel-eslint is deprecated, replaced by @babel/eslint-parser
findings.description: babel-eslint ^10.1.0 was deprecated in March 2020 and renamed to @babel/eslint-parser. It no longer receives updates, bug fixes, or security patches.
findings.recommendation: Replace babel-eslint with @babel/eslint-parser in package.json devDependencies. Update .eslintrc to use parser: '@babel/eslint-parser'. This can be done independently of the Ember upgrade.

findings.title: eslint-plugin-ember 6.x is severely outdated
findings.description: eslint-plugin-ember ^6.2.0 is installed. The latest version is 12.x+. Versions 6.x lack rules for Ember Octane patterns (native classes, tracked properties, Glimmer components) that the project claims to use (edition: 'octane' in package.json).
findings.recommendation: Upgrade eslint-plugin-ember to the latest version compatible with the project's ESLint version. This will improve linting coverage for Octane-era patterns.

findings.title: Bower dependencies are abandoned and a supply chain risk
findings.description: The project uses bower.json with 9 dependencies including moment ~2.9.0 (from 2015, current is 2.30+), bootstrap ~3.3.2 (from 2015, current is 5.3+), and several packages pinned to wildcard '*' versions (recordrtc, IndexedDBShim, wordcloud2.js, hammer-time, qrcode-js). Bower itself has been deprecated since 2017. Wildcard version pins (*) mean any version could be installed, creating severe supply chain risk.
findings.recommendation: Migrate all bower dependencies to npm packages managed through package.json. Replace wildcard '*' versions with explicit version pins. Moment.js should be replaced with a lighter alternative like date-fns or dayjs. Bootstrap 3.x should be evaluated for upgrade to 5.x or replaced.

findings.title: No lock file for frontend npm dependencies
findings.description: Neither yarn.lock nor package-lock.json was found in app/frontend/. Without a lock file, every npm/yarn install can resolve to different dependency versions, making builds non-reproducible and vulnerable to supply chain attacks where a malicious version could be pulled.
findings.recommended_version: Generate and commit a lock file
findings.recommendation: Run 'npm install' or 'yarn install' in app/frontend/ to generate a lock file. Commit the resulting package-lock.json or yarn.lock to version control. This is critical for build reproducibility and supply chain security.

findings.title: ttfunk is pinned to exact version 1.7 (5+ years old)
findings.description: ttfunk is hard-pinned to version 1.7.0 in the Gemfile (released circa 2019). This is required by the prawn gem (used via obf for PDF generation). The latest ttfunk is 1.8.x. Hard pinning prevents security updates.
findings.recommendation: Test if prawn 2.4.0 works with ttfunk 1.8.x. If so, change the pin from '1.7' to '~> 1.7' or '~> 1.8'. The hard pin was likely added to resolve a specific compatibility issue that may now be fixed.

findings.title: Several niche/low-download gems with single maintainers
findings.description: The project depends on several niche gems that appear to have single maintainers and low download counts: go_secure (0.70), boy_band (0.1.16), permissable-coughdrop (0.3.4), accessible-books (0.1.1), and obf (0.9.9.3). These are likely CoughDrop ecosystem gems. Single-maintainer gems are a supply chain risk -- if the maintainer becomes unavailable, security patches cannot be released.
findings.recommendation: Since LingoLinq forked from CoughDrop, consider forking these gems into the LingoLinq organization's GitHub. The TODO in the Gemfile already notes republishing permissable-coughdrop as permissable-lingolinq. Vendoring ensures control over security patches.

findings.title: Project is AGPLv3 -- all dependencies must be AGPL-compatible
findings.description: The project uses AGPLv3 license. All dependencies must be compatible (MIT, BSD, Apache 2.0, ISC, LGPL are compatible; proprietary or incompatible copyleft licenses are not). The frontend package.json also declares AGPLv3. Most Ruby gems in the ecosystem use MIT license. No incompatible licenses were found among top-level dependencies, but the s3 gem and some bower components should be verified.
findings.recommendation: Perform a full license scan using 'license_finder' gem or 'licensed' tool. Pay special attention to the niche CoughDrop-ecosystem gems (go_secure, boy_band, permissable-coughdrop, accessible-books, obf) as their licenses may not be clearly documented.

findings.title: qunit-dom 0.8.4 is extremely outdated
findings.description: qunit-dom ^0.8.4 is specified in devDependencies. The latest version is 3.x. While this is a test-only dependency, newer versions provide better assertions and DOM testing helpers for Ember tests.
findings.recommendation: Upgrade as part of the Ember modernization effort.

findings.title: ember-cli-eslint is deprecated in favor of direct eslint integration
findings.description: ember-cli-eslint ^5.1.0 is an Ember addon that wraps ESLint. It was deprecated in favor of running ESLint directly. Modern Ember projects do not use this addon.
findings.recommendation: Remove ember-cli-eslint and run eslint directly via npm scripts. This is a low-priority cleanup item.

findings.title: ember-cli-template-lint is deprecated in favor of ember-template-lint
findings.description: ember-cli-template-lint ^2.0.2 is an Ember addon wrapper for template linting. It was deprecated and replaced by direct use of ember-template-lint.
findings.recommendation: Replace with direct ember-template-lint usage. Low priority.

findings.title: Several Gemfile dependencies lack version constraints
findings.description: Multiple gems in the Gemfile have no version constraints: typhoeus, coffee-rails, aws-sdk-rails, resque, puma, geokit, obf, accessible-books, s3, bugsnag, stripe, rack-attack, newrelic_rpm, rack-timeout, pg_search, silencer, go_secure, boy_band, permissable-coughdrop, ruby-saml, rotp, sinatra, sanitize. This means 'bundle update' could pull in breaking major version changes.
findings.recommendation: Add pessimistic version constraints (~>) to all gems in the Gemfile. For example: gem 'puma', '~> 7.1', gem 'stripe', '~> 18.0', etc. The Gemfile.lock prevents accidental upgrades, but explicit constraints document intent and prevent surprises.

findings.title: Puma 7.1.0 should be verified for latest patches
findings.description: Puma 7.1.0 is installed. CVE-2024-45614 (header clobbering) was fixed in 6.4.3/5.6.9. Puma 7.1.0 should include this fix, but verify the latest 7.x release for any additional security patches.
findings.recommendation: Run 'bundle update puma' to get the latest 7.x release. Puma 7.1.0 should include fixes for CVE-2024-45614 but verify.

findings.title: ember-maybe-import-regenerator is unnecessary with modern Babel
findings.description: ember-maybe-import-regenerator ^0.1.6 conditionally imports the regenerator-runtime polyfill for async/await. With Ember CLI Babel 7.26+ and modern browser targets, this is no longer needed.
findings.recommendation: Remove from devDependencies if browser targets no longer include IE11. This reduces bundle size.

findings.title: ember-cli-update listed as production dependency instead of dev
findings.description: ember-cli-update ^0.48.2 is listed under 'dependencies' instead of 'devDependencies' in package.json. This is a development-only tool for upgrading Ember projects and should not be in the production bundle.
findings.recommendation: Move ember-cli-update from 'dependencies' to 'devDependencies' in package.json.

findings.title: pg_search 2.3.7 is behind latest 2.x
findings.description: pg_search 2.3.7 is installed. The latest version may include performance improvements and bug fixes for PostgreSQL full-text search.
findings.recommendation: Run 'bundle update pg_search' to get the latest 2.x release.

findings.title: postinstall-postinstall is a workaround package that may mask issues
findings.description: postinstall-postinstall ^2.1.0 ensures the postinstall script runs after 'yarn remove' operations. This is a workaround for a yarn v1 bug and indicates the project may be using yarn v1.
findings.recommendation: If using npm instead of yarn, this package is unnecessary. Remove if not using yarn v1.

findings.title: Moment.js 2.9.x (bower) has known ReDoS and prototype pollution issues
findings.description: The bower.json pins moment to ~2.9.0, which is from 2015. Moment.js versions prior to 2.29.2 have known ReDoS vulnerabilities. Additionally, Moment.js is in maintenance mode and the maintainers recommend alternatives for new projects.
findings.recommended_version: Replace with dayjs or date-fns via npm
findings.recommendation: Migrate moment from bower to an npm package, upgrading to at least 2.29.4 or replacing with a modern alternative like dayjs (which has a moment-compatible API). The ~2.9.0 version is 7 years behind and has known vulnerabilities.

findings.title: lodash and merge in devDependencies appear to be security overrides, not direct dependencies
findings.description: lodash '>=4.17.11' and merge '>=1.2.1' use >= constraints in devDependencies, which is unusual. These are likely added to force-resolve known vulnerabilities in transitive dependencies rather than being directly used. This pattern can mask the actual vulnerable transitive dependency.
findings.recommendation: Move these to the 'overrides' section of package.json (already done for ember-cli-babel). This makes the intent clearer and is the modern approach to forcing transitive dependency versions.

findings.title: Bootstrap 3.3.x (bower) is end of life
findings.description: Bootstrap ~3.3.2 is installed via bower. Bootstrap 3 reached end of life in July 2019. It no longer receives security patches. Bootstrap 3 has known XSS vulnerabilities in certain tooltip/popover configurations (CVE-2019-8331, CVE-2018-14042, CVE-2018-14041, CVE-2018-14040).
findings.recommendation: Migrate Bootstrap from bower to npm and upgrade to Bootstrap 5.x. This is a significant effort due to CSS/JS API changes between 3.x and 5.x, so plan it alongside the Ember upgrade.

priority_actions.action: Update ruby-saml to latest patched version to address critical authentication bypass CVEs
Update rack to 2.2.22 to patch path traversal and DoS vulnerabilities
Update nokogiri to 1.19.1+ to fix canonicalization vulnerability
Update sinatra to patch ReDoS and open redirect vulnerabilities
Generate and commit a package-lock.json or yarn.lock for the frontend
Remove rack-offline (abandoned, uses defunct browser API)
Remove rails_12factor (unnecessary since Rails 5 and Render migration)
Replace s3 gem with aws-sdk-s3 for consistency and security
Update ruby-openai from 6.x to 8.x for latest API support
Plan and begin Ember 3.28 -> 4.x upgrade to exit EOL framework

score_explanation: Score of 38/100 reflects: 4 high/critical vulnerability findings (-30), EOL frontend framework (-15), 6 unmaintained dependencies (-10), missing lock file (-5), 22 unpinned gems (-2). The project's Ruby backend dependencies are in reasonable shape (Rails 7.2.3, Ruby 3.4.4 are current), but the frontend (Ember 3.28, bower, ESLint 5) and several abandoned gems (rack-offline, s3, coffee-rails, rails_12factor) significantly drag down the score.
